title: Annual Report 2023
summary: The Bank of Canada's Annual Report 2023 landing page provides only a brief excerpt from the Governor's foreword and section navigation links. The extracted content states that in 2023 the Bank made progress fighting inflation while acknowledging cost-of-living pressures on Canadians, and references global uncertainty from foreign bank failures and geopolitical conflicts.
key_points: Bank of Canada describes 2023 as a year of considerable progress in fighting inflation while acknowledging ongoing cost-of-living pressures on Canadians
The Bank used its policy interest rate as the primary tool to control inflation
The Bank's challenge was to set rates high enough to reduce inflation but not so high as to excessively harm the economy
Global risks including foreign bank failures and the Israel-Gaza war contributed to economic uncertainty in 2023
The Bank also engaged in resilience partnerships to promote operational and cyber resilience of the financial sector
why_it_matters: This landing page does not contain substantive policy content. The actual Annual Report PDF would need to be retrieved for meaningful archival value. The page serves as an index to the full report sections but provides minimal extractable information.
macro_context: The limited extracted content references the 2023 macro environment where the Bank of Canada pursued restrictive monetary policy to reduce inflation, but provides no specific data points or policy details.
